aquacroptools 0.1.0
  • Reference

Initialize AquaCrop Project Structure

Source: R/init_aquacrop.R
init_aquacrop.Rd

Creates a directory structure for AquaCrop crop water productivity model simulations, including folders for climate data, crop parameters, soil profiles, and simulation outputs. Copies the AquaCrop executable and documentation files from the package installation.

Usage

init_aquacrop(
  path = ".",
  copy_exe = TRUE,
  copy_docs = TRUE,
  copy_templates = TRUE,
  overwrite = FALSE
)

Arguments

path

Character string specifying the root directory where the project should be created. Default is the current working directory (".").

copy_exe

Logical; if TRUE (default), copies the AquaCrop executable from the package to the project directory.

copy_docs

Logical; if TRUE (default), copies LICENSE and AUTHORS.md files from the package.

copy_templates

Logical; if TRUE (default), copies template simulation files to the SIMUL/ directory.

overwrite

Logical; if TRUE, overwrites existing directory structure. Default: FALSE.

Value

Invisibly returns the normalized path to the created directory.

Details

The function creates the following directory structure:

  • CLIMATE/ - Climate input files (temperature, rainfall, ETo)

  • CROP/ - Crop parameter files

  • LIST/ - Project and simulation list files

  • MANAGEMENT/ - Field management practice files

  • OUTP/ - Simulation output files

  • PARAM/ - General parameter files

  • SIMUL/ - Simulation configuration files

  • SOIL/ - Soil profile and characteristic files

The appropriate AquaCrop executable for your operating system (Windows, macOS, or Linux) is automatically copied from the package installation.

Template simulation files are copied to the SIMUL/ directory:

  • AggregationResults.SIM - Aggregation output configuration

  • DailyResultsFullList.SIM - Daily results output configuration

  • MaunaLoa.CO2 - CO2 concentration data

  • ParticularResultsFullList.SIM - Particular results output configuration

References

https://www.fao.org/aquacrop/ for AquaCrop documentation

See also

has_aquacrop, get_aquacrop_path

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Initialize in current directory
init_aquacrop()

# Initialize in specific location
init_aquacrop("C:/Projects/MyAquaCrop")

# Initialize without copying executable
init_aquacrop("./my_project", copy_exe = FALSE)

# Initialize without template files
init_aquacrop("./my_project", copy_templates = FALSE)

# Overwrite existing structure
init_aquacrop("./existing_project", overwrite = TRUE)
} # }
